interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a hump, with the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeding the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 15Y peaks at 12.68%. The 364D dip below 182D suggests near-term liquidity unevenness. Yields above the 10% repo rate reflect tight banking sector liquidity and persistent inflationary pressures. The 2Y bulge indicates market expectations of prolonged BB tightening before eventual easing, with banks competing aggressively for term funding amid stressed advance-deposit ratios.
implications: Elevated short-to-medium term yields signal banks face acute liquidity stress, with AD ratios constraining lending capacity and driving up government borrowing costs
The 2Y hump above longer tenors suggests the market prices BB maintaining hawkish policy through FY26 to anchor inflation expectations before a gradual easing cycle

The steep 15Y-20Y segment and wide 10Y-91D spread (130bps) indicate fiscal supply concerns from growing budget deficits and investor demand for significant term premia on longer-dated sovereign exposure
